preview_text:
The model gives Germany a marginal edge at 35.2%, but the contest is tightly bunched. Ecuador registers 33.2% to win, leaving a gap of just 2 percentage points between the two outright favorites. This suggests a genuinely competitive matchup rather than a heavily tilted affair.

The draw probability of 31.5% sits meaningfully above either team's win chance. The spread across all three outcomes is narrow, indicating the model expects a close and unpredictable encounter. Ecuador's 33.2% win probability in particular reflects a scenario where the underdog poses genuine difficulty, even if Germany holds a slight edge in the model's assessment.

The market consensus diverges notably from the model. Sportsbooks (38 books, vig-stripped) assign Germany 54.8%, a gap of roughly 20 percentage points above the model. Polymarket prices align closely with the books, reflecting similar sentiment. The dashboard does not claim to know which view is correct; the disparity suggests material disagreement about Ecuador's competitive standing in this fixture.
